---
name: tos-web-ui-design
description: Design patterns for TelemetryOS web mount point views. Use when building a browser-accessible interface (operator desk, public form, staff dashboard) that lives outside the device display and admin settings. Covers routing, entity-driven navigation, store access, and styling.
---

# Web Mount Point Design

The web mount point is a third interface alongside Render (device display) and Settings (admin config). It runs in a regular browser — not embedded in Studio, not on a device — and can be private (staff/operators) or publicly available.

> **Prerequisites:** Read `tos-architecture` for mount point fundamentals and `tos-multi-mode` for entity-scoped data patterns. The web mount point always pairs with multi-mode architecture.

---

## What Is the Web Mount Point?

```
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│   Any Browser (Phone/Tablet/    │
│   Desktop — Staff or Public)    │
│                                 │
│  ┌───────────────────────────┐  │
│  │   TelemetryOS Web Host    │  │
│  │  ┌─────────────────────┐  │  │
│  │  │    Your App         │  │  │
│  │  │   /app-name route   │  │  │
│  │  │                     │  │  │
│  │  │  Operator desk,     │  │  │
│  │  │  public form, staff │  │  │
│  │  │  dashboard          │  │  │
│  │  └─────────────────────┘  │  │
│  └───────────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Use for:**
- Operator desks (staff calling queue numbers from their phone)
- Staff dashboards and management consoles
- Public signup forms or registration pages
- Public status displays or reporting views

**Key characteristics:**
- Runs in any browser, accessible via URL
- Can be staff-facing (private) or public-facing
- Multi-level navigation with React Router
- Application and dynamic namespace store scopes only
- **No access to device or instance store** (no device/instance context)
- Standard HTML/CSS — no SDK settings components, no UI scaling
- Fixed base font size (no `useUiScaleToSetRem()`)

---

## telemetry.config.json

The web mount point uses the app name as its route path:

```json
{
  "name": "my-app",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "useSpaRouting": true,
  "mountPoints": {
    "render": "/render",
    "settings": "/settings",
    "web": "/my-app"
  },
  "devServer": {
    "runCommand": "vite --port 3000",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000"
  }
}
```

**Rules:**
- Web mount point path uses the app name: `"web": "/my-app"`
- `useSpaRouting: true` is required (web views use client-side routing)
- Render and Settings keep their standard paths

---

## Routing Architecture

### App Name as Base Path

All web routes live under the app name:

```typescript
// App.tsx
import { createBrowserRouter, RouterProvider } from 'react-router'

const router = createBrowserRouter([
  // Standard mount points
  { path: '/render', Component: Render },
  { path: '/settings', Component: Settings },

  // Web mount point routes — all under /my-app
  { path: '/my-app', Component: WebEntities },
  { path: '/my-app/locations/:locationName', Component: WebLocationDetail },
  { path: '/my-app/locations/:locationName/counters/:counterId', Component: WebCounter },
])
```

### Entity-Driven URL Hierarchy

The organizing entity from your multi-mode architecture drives the URL structure. Choose path segments that are meaningful for your app domain:

```
/my-app                                              → List all entities
/my-app/locations/:locationName                      → Entity detail / sub-items
/my-app/locations/:locationName/counters/:counterId  → Deep detail view
```

**Path segments are app-specific.** Pick names that match your domain:

| App Domain | Example Paths |
|------------|---------------|
| Queue / service | `/locations/:name/counters/:id` |
| Content / editorial | `/topics/:slug/articles/:id` |
| Event / scheduling | `/events/:name/sessions/:id` |
| Retail / menu | `/stores/:name/categories/:id` |

### URL Encoding

Entity names may contain spaces or special characters. Always encode and decode:

```typescript
// Creating links
<Link to={`/my-app/locations/${encodeURIComponent(location)}`}>

// Reading params
const { locationName } = useParams()
const decoded = decodeURIComponent(locationName || '')
```

---

## View Hierarchy Pattern

Web views follow a drill-down pattern from entity list to detail:

### Root View — Entity List

Lists all organizing entities from **application-scoped** store:

```typescript
import { Link } from 'react-router'
import { useLocationsStoreState } from '../hooks/store'

export function WebEntities() {
  const [isLoading, locations] = useLocationsStoreState()

  if (isLoading) return <div className="web web--loading">Loading...</div>

  const safeLocations = locations || []

  return (
    <div className="web">
      <h1>My App</h1>
      <p>Select a location</p>
      <nav>
        {safeLocations.map(loc => (
          <Link
            key={loc}
            to={`/my-app/locations/${encodeURIComponent(loc)}`}
          >
            {loc}
          </Link>
        ))}
      </nav>
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Entity View — Scoped Sub-Items

Reads entity name from URL params, derives namespace, shows entity-scoped data:

```typescript
import { Link, useParams } from 'react-router'
import { useLocationsStoreState, useCountersStoreState, locationNamespace } from '../hooks/store'

export function WebLocationDetail() {
  const { locationName } = useParams()
  const decoded = decodeURIComponent(locationName || '')

  const [isLoadingLocations, locations] = useLocationsStoreState()
  const ns = locationNamespace(decoded)
  const [isLoadingCounters, counters] = useCountersStoreState(ns, 250)

  const isLoading = isLoadingLocations || isLoadingCounters
  if (isLoading) return <div className="web web--loading">Loading...</div>

  // Validate entity still exists
  if (!locations?.includes(decoded)) {
    return (
      <div className="web">
        <h1>Not Found</h1>
        <Link to="/my-app">Back</Link>
      </div>
    )
  }

  return (
    <div className="web">
      <Link to="/my-app">Back</Link>
      <h1>{decoded}</h1>
      <nav>
        {(counters || []).map(counter => (
          <Link
            key={counter.id}
            to={`/my-app/locations/${encodeURIComponent(decoded)}/counters/${counter.id}`}
          >
            {counter.name}
          </Link>
        ))}
      </nav>
    </div>
  )
}
```

### Detail View — Full Interaction

The deepest view where users perform actions. Full read-write access to namespace-scoped store:

```typescript
export function WebCounter() {
  const { locationName, counterId } = useParams()
  const navigate = useNavigate()
  const decoded = decodeURIComponent(locationName || '')

  const ns = locationNamespace(decoded)

  // Load all entity-scoped data
  const [isLoadingST, serviceTypes, setServiceTypes] = useServiceTypesStoreState(ns, 250)
  const [isLoadingC, counters, setCounters] = useCountersStoreState(ns, 250)

  // ... operator actions that call setServiceTypes(), setCounters(), etc.
  // Changes sync to Render views in real-time via shared store
}
```

---

## Store Access

### Available Scopes

| Scope | Available | Use |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| Application | Yes | Entity lists, API keys, account-wide config |
| Dynamic namespace | Yes | Entity-scoped shared data |
| Instance | **No** | Tied to device/instance pair — no context in web |
| Device | **No** | Tied to physical device — no context in web |

### Entity from URL, Not Instance Store

This is the key architectural difference from Render:

- **Render** gets the selected entity from **instance store** (`useSelectedLocationStoreState`) — admin picks which entity this device shows
- **Web** gets the entity from **URL params** (`useParams()`) — user navigates to the entity via links

Both derive the **same namespace** using the same helper function, so they share the same data:

```typescript
// In Render — entity from instance store
const [isLoading, selectedLocation] = useSelectedLocationStoreState()
if (isLoading) return <div>Loading...</div>
const ns = locationNamespace(selectedLocation || 'Location A')

// In Web — entity from URL params
const { locationName } = useParams()
const ns = locationNamespace(decodeURIComponent(locationName || ''))

// Same namespace → same data → real-time sync between Render and Web
```

---

## Styling

### No UI Scaling

The base template sets `font-size: 1vmax` on `html` for viewport-relative scaling on Render/Settings. Web views run in a regular browser and need standard sizing — use `html:has(.web)` to neutralize the scaling and restore the browser default `16px`. Do NOT use `useUiScaleToSetRem()`:

```css
/* Web.css — neutralize the 1vmax UI scaling used by Render/Settings */
html:has(.web) {
  font-size: 16px;
}

.web {
  font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
  line-height: 1.5;
  color: #111;
  padding: 1rem;
}
```

### Standard Web Design

- Use standard CSS — no SDK settings components
- Design for responsive web (phones, tablets, desktops)
- Use `px`, `rem`, or `em` units at normal web scale
- Consider mobile-first if operators use phones
- Apply familiar web UX patterns (navigation, forms, buttons)

---

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── App.tsx             # Router — includes web routes
├── views/
│   ├── Render.tsx      # /render mount point
│   ├── Settings.tsx    # /settings mount point
│   ├── Web.tsx         # /app-name mount point (all web views)
│   └── Web.css         # Web view styles
├── hooks/
│   └── store.ts        # Shared store hooks (used across all mount points)
└── types/
    └── app.ts          # Shared types
```

For complex apps with many web views, split into a directory:

```
src/views/
├── Web/
│   ├── WebEntities.tsx
│   ├── WebDetail.tsx
│   ├── WebOperator.tsx
│   └── Web.css
```

---

## Common Mistakes

| Mistake | Problem | Fix |
|---------|---------|-----|
| Using instance/device store hooks | Throws error or returns nothing — no device/instance context | Use only application and dynamic namespace store hooks |
| Using `useUiScaleToSetRem()` | Unnecessary — web views aren't on TV displays | Use fixed CSS font sizing |
| Using SDK settings components | Won't style correctly outside Studio | Use standard HTML/CSS |
| Hardcoding entity in web views | Breaks navigation, can't select different entities | Get entity from URL params via `useParams()` |
| Forgetting `useSpaRouting: true` | Routes break on page refresh | Add to telemetry.config.json |
| Not URL-encoding entity names | Breaks on names with spaces/special chars | Always use `encodeURIComponent`/`decodeURIComponent` |
| Not validating entity exists | Crash if entity was removed by admin | Check entity against application-scoped list, show "not found" |

---

## Checklist

Before implementing a web view, confirm:

- [ ] telemetry.config.json has `"web": "/app-name"` and `useSpaRouting: true`
- [ ] App.tsx routes all web paths under the app name
- [ ] Web views only use application and dynamic namespace store hooks
- [ ] Entity is read from URL params (not instance store)
- [ ] Namespace is derived using the same helper function as Render
- [ ] Entity names are URL-encoded in links and decoded in components
- [ ] Entity existence is validated against application-scoped list
- [ ] Back navigation exists at each level
- [ ] No `useUiScaleToSetRem()` or SDK settings components used

---
